mode.title: Mode
mode.serverSummary: Local MCP / CLI server is available on port {port}.
mode.managedClientRunning: Managed client runtime is polling the remote backend.
mode.managedClientMcpWsRunning: Managed client runtime is connected to the remote desktop WebSocket endpoint.
mode.managedClientWaiting: Managed client runtime is enabled and waiting for setup to start.
mode.closeHint: Closing the window only hides it to the tray. Use tray Quit to fully stop the app.
mode.closeHintManaged: Closing the window does not stop managed client polling. Use tray Quit to fully stop the client.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.pullInactiveHint: Available when running in managed client mode.
dashboard.pullWaitingMcpWs: Connected and waiting for tool calls
dashboard.connectionStateSigninHint: Sign in to establish the bridge
dashboard.connectionIdPending: Connection ID will appear after the bridge is established.
dashboard.noRunningSessions: No live local sessions right now.
dashboard.noSessions: No sessions yet. Use MCP tools or WebSocket to execute commands.
dashboard.clearHistoryRequiresSignin: Sign in first before clearing history in managed MCP WebSocket mode.

builtInTools.title: Built-in Tools
builtInTools.description: Manage local safety rules for the built-in shell and file read tools exposed by the desktop client.
builtInTools.currentPermissionProfileDescription: Permission profile selection is managed on the dedicated Permissions page. This page is now for advanced per-tool restrictions and overrides.
builtInTools.permissionProfileDescription: Choose the remote exposure model first. The profile sets which desktop tools are published upstream and how much successful tool output can leave the machine.
builtInTools.permissionProfileCommandOnlySummary: Remote side can trigger local commands, but successful tool calls only return success status. File reads, output reads, and all external MCP servers stay blocked.
builtInTools.permissionProfileInteractiveTrustedSumma: Remote side can use a small interactive handle surface such as session_create and session_wait, but successful results still avoid returning command output or file contents.
builtInTools.permissionProfileFullLocalAdminSummary: Full local tool surface, including unrestricted file reads and both HTTP and stdio external MCP servers.
builtInTools.permissionProfileOverridesActive: This profile currently has manual overrides below. Saving will persist only the differences from the selected profile.
builtInTools.permissionProfileNoOverrides: The current settings match the selected profile defaults.
builtInTools.loadFailed: Failed to load built-in tool configuration, showing defaults: {error}
builtInTools.saveApplied: Built-in tool rules saved and republished with {toolCount} tool(s).
builtInTools.savePendingPublishRuntimeInactive: Built-in tool rules saved, but managed MCP WebSocket mode is not currently running, so tools were not republished.
builtInTools.savePendingPublishBridgeNotReady: Built-in tool rules saved, but the managed MCP WebSocket bridge is not ready to republish tools yet. The latest rules will be used on the next successful connection.
builtInTools.savePendingPublishRepublishPending: Built-in tool rules were saved. Tool republish is still running in the background, so the UI returned immediately instead of waiting for slow external MCP servers.
builtInTools.saveFailed: Failed to save built-in tool rules: {error}
builtInTools.invalidNumericConfig: All numeric limits must be positive integers.
builtInTools.allowlistTooltip: Empty allowlist = not tightened. Non-empty allowlist = strict enforcement.
builtInTools.shellAllowlistHint: Use allowlists as the primary boundary. In managed-client-mcp-ws mode, shell_execute requires at least one allowed executable name.
builtInTools.shellRecommendedTemplateHint: Recommended starter template: common project tools such as git, node, npm, npx, and python.
builtInTools.shellAllowlistRequiredManagedMcpWs: In managed-client-mcp-ws mode, shell_execute requires at least one allowed executable name.
builtInTools.fileReadAllowlistHint: Use allowed read roots as the primary boundary. In full-local-admin mode, file_read requires at least one allowed read root.
builtInTools.fileReadRecommendedTemplateHint: Recommended starter template: the current managed workspace root and current working directory.
builtInTools.fileReadAllowlistRequiredFullLocalAdmin: In full-local-admin mode, file_read requires at least one allowed read root.
builtInTools.fileReadProfileRestricted: File read is reserved for full-local-admin. In command-only and interactive-trusted modes, successful tool calls must not return local file contents to the remote side.
builtInTools.managedMcpServerAdminDescription: Enable the built-in desktop tool that allows trusted remote instructions to create or update external MCP server configuration on this node.
builtInTools.managedMcpServerAdminProfileRestricted: Managed MCP server administration is reserved for full-local-admin because it expands the node's remote attack surface.
builtInTools.managedMcpServerAdminNote: This publishes the managed_mcp_server_upsert tool upstream in managed MCP WebSocket mode. HTTP is lower risk than stdio because stdio can launch local processes.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: Choose the remote exposure model for this desktop node before tuning individual tool restrictions.
permissions.previewDescription: Selecting a permission profile updates the live preview below so you can see which desktop tools will be published upstream and which ones stay blocked.
permissions.emptyExternalMcpList: No matching MCP servers in the current saved configuration.
permissions.externalMcpBlockedTransport: Blocked by the selected profile transport policy.

transportDescription.http: Connects to an external MCP server over HTTP.
transportDescription.stdio: Launches or talks to an external MCP server through standard input/output.

toolResultModeDescription.status-only: Only status metadata is returned. Output content is not returned directly.
toolResultModeDescription.handle: Returns a handle or session reference. Use follow-up tools to read details.
toolResultModeDescription.full: Returns the full tool result, including the main output payload.

permissions.advancedDescription: Detailed shell, file read, and managed MCP server restrictions remain on the Built-in Tools page.

settings.title: Settings
settings.mcpDescription: Use this URL to connect AI agents or VS Code MCP clients to this server.
settings.notificationsDescription: Control system notifications for new sessions and inbound managed tool calls.
settings.accountDescription: Sign out of the current managed MCP WebSocket session on this device.
settings.workspaceDescription: Managed MCP WebSocket mode writes generated files to the current run directory and moves the previous run into the archive directory on restart.
settings.signInConfigMissing: Managed MCP WebSocket base URL is not configured yet.
settings.externalMcpDescription: Configure additional MCP servers and publish their tools through the managed MCP WebSocket client.
settings.externalMcpPermissionsDescription: Permission profile selection is managed on the dedicated Permissions page.
settings.externalMcpAllowedTransportsCommandOnly: External MCP publication is disabled in command-only mode.
settings.externalMcpAllowedTransportsInteractiveT: External MCP publication stays disabled in interactive-trusted mode. Only the small built-in interactive handle surface is allowed.
settings.externalMcpAllowedTransportsFullLocalAdm: HTTP and stdio external MCP transports can become active in this profile.
settings.externalMcpEffectiveSummary: Effective state: {active} active, {blocked} blocked by policy, {disabled} disabled.
settings.externalMcpLiveHint: Changes are pushed upstream with update_tools while managed MCP WebSocket mode is running.
settings.externalMcpInactiveHint: Changes are saved now and will publish the next time managed MCP WebSocket mode connects.
settings.externalMcpEmpty: No external MCP servers configured yet.
settings.externalMcpRequiredPermissionProfileHint: This server requires at least {required}. The current node profile is {current}.
settings.externalMcpTools: Allowed Tools (one per line, * for all)
settings.externalMcpTestBlocked: Test skipped. {reason} Required profile: {required}. Current profile: {current}.
settings.externalMcpSaveApplied: Configuration saved and update_tools published with {toolCount} tool(s).
settings.externalMcpSaveOnly: Configuration saved. Tools will publish when managed MCP WebSocket mode is active.
settings.externalMcpRefreshSkipped: Managed MCP WebSocket mode is not active, so update_tools was not sent.
settings.securityNotice: Security guardrails will be available in a future release. For now, the server accepts all connections and commands without restriction. Use with caution.
settings.aboutDescription: CLI Server provides a local WebSocket + HTTP gateway for web-based AI agents to execute CLI commands on your machine. It runs a persistent background service on the configured port and logs all command executions for audit purposes.
settings.languageDescription: Interface language (requires restart for tray menu)
